briefing:
# Atlantic Fintech Daily Briefing
### Tuesday, March 24, 2026

---

## 🌍 Top 5 Global Fintech News

- **[Fintech founder charged with fraud; AI app found to be humans in the Philippines](https://techcrunch.com/2025/04/10/fintech-founder-charged-with-fraud-after-ai-shopping-app-found-to-be-powered-by-humans-in-the-philippines/)** — *Hacker News / TechCrunch* — A fintech founder faces fraud charges after their AI-powered shopping app was revealed to actually be operated by human workers in the Philippines.

- **[Americans see their savings vanish in Synapse fintech crisis](https://www.cnbc.com/2024/11/22/synapse-bankruptcy-thousands-of-americans-see-their-savings-vanish.html)** — *Hacker News / CNBC* — Thousands of Americans lost access to their savings following the bankruptcy of fintech middleware provider Synapse, highlighting systemic risks in the BaaS model.

- **[Fintech is making credit cards weirder](https://workweek.com/2022/08/05/fintech-is-making-credit-cards-weirder/)** — *Hacker News / Workweek* — Fintech innovation is pushing credit card products into increasingly niche and unconventional territory, reshaping how consumers think about credit.

- **[Apple acquires UK open banking startup Credit Kudos](https://ffnews.com/newsarticle/apple-acquires-uk-open-banking-startup-credit-kudos/)** — *Hacker News / FF News* — Apple's acquisition of open banking startup Credit Kudos signals the tech giant's growing ambitions in financial data and alternative credit decisioning.

- **[Open Banking and Payments Competition](https://www.bitsaboutmoney.com/archive/open-banking-and-payments-competition/)** — *Hacker News / Bits About Money* — A deep-dive analysis explores how open banking frameworks are reshaping competition in the payments landscape globally.

---

## 🍁 Top 5 Canadian Fintech News

- **[Canada's moment to build is now](https://betakit.com/canadas-moment-to-build-is-now/)** — *BetaKit* — A timely opinion piece argues that Canada must seize the current geopolitical and economic moment to accelerate its domestic tech and innovation ecosystem.

- **[Canada calls screen scraping 'unsecure,' sets Open Banking target for 2023](https://finledger.com/2021/08/09/canada-sets-open-banking-target-for-2023/)** — *Hacker News / FinLedger* — Canada's federal government flagged screen scraping as a security risk and set an open banking implementation target, a milestone relevant to understanding where Canada's open banking journey stands today.

- **[BKR Capital secures initial $20 million for second Black Innovation Fund](https://betakit.com/bkr-capital-secures-initial-20-million-for-second-black-innovation-fund/)** — *BetaKit* — BKR Capital has closed the first tranche of its second Black Innovation Fund, reinforcing Canada's commitment to inclusive venture capital and diverse founder ecosystems.

- **[UBC and InBC launch new fund to support university spinouts](https://betakit.com/ubc-and-inbc-launch-new-fund-to-support-university-spinouts/)** — *BetaKit* — UBC and InBC Investment Corp. have partnered to launch a new fund aimed at commercializing research and supporting university-born startups in British Columbia.

- **[Spark Microsystems secures additional $17 million in Series B funding](https://betakit.com/spark-microsystems-secures-additional-17-million-in-series-b-funding/)** — *BetaKit* — Montreal-based Spark Microsystems has closed an additional $17M in Series B funding to advance its ultra-low-power wireless semiconductor technology.

---

## 🌊 Bonus: Relevant to Atlantic Fintech's Focus (1–3 articles)

- **[BKR Capital secures initial $20 million for second Black Innovation Fund](https://betakit.com/bkr-capital-secures-initial-20-million-for-second-black-innovation-fund/)** — *BetaKit* — As Atlantic Fintech champions financial inclusion and ecosystem building, this fund's focus on underrepresented founders offers a model for similar inclusive capital initiatives in Atlantic Canada.

- **[Canada's moment to build is now](https://betakit.com/canadas-moment-to-build-is-now/)** — *BetaKit* — The call for Canada to accelerate innovation and domestic tech growth resonates strongly for Atlantic Canada, where ecosystem builders are working to position the region as a competitive fintech hub.

- **[Open Banking and Payments Competition](https://www.bitsaboutmoney.com/archive/open-banking-and-payments-competition/)** — *Hacker News / Bits About Money* — With open banking remaining a central focus for Atlantic Fintech and Canadian credit unions, this analysis of how open banking drives payments competition is essential reading for regional stakeholders.

---

*📌 Note: Today's feed contained limited fintech-specific Canadian news. Some global articles with high Canadian relevance (particularly on open banking) were included in the Canadian section with context noted. Atlantic Fintech will continue monitoring for breaking regional news.*
